title: Wix Seller Financing
secondaryKeywords: wix funding,wix business financing,ecommerce seller cash flow funding,online store working capital
h1: Wix Seller Financing for Growing Online Stores
heroSubhead: Bridge inventory, marketing, and fulfillment timing gaps with funding aligned to how Wix sellers operate.
heroImageAlt: Wix seller reviewing online orders and inventory plans
industryContext: Wix sellers often manage uneven cash cycles where inventory and acquisition costs come first and revenue settles later. Platform fees, returns, and fulfillment overhead can tighten available working capital even during growth periods. Funding can help stabilize operations between sales cycles so teams can maintain inventory availability, customer service, and marketing continuity.
financingNeedsIntro: Common Wix seller funding uses include: - Inventory reorders before peak demand`n- Paid acquisition campaign spend`n- Fulfillment and shipping cost coverage`n- Working capital for payroll and contractors
qualificationIntro: Applicants should prepare recent business bank statements, revenue history, and a clear use-of-funds summary. Including seasonality context and major expense categories helps support a cleaner review process.
processOverview: Typical review flow: 1. Submit a short application.`n2. Provide requested business and financial documents.`n3. Review available terms if qualified.`n4. Complete acceptance and final funding steps. Timing varies by documentation completeness and underwriting requirements.
disclosureBlock: Distilled Funding by Moonshine Capital provides business funding options for qualifying businesses. Eligibility, offer structure, cost, and timing depend on submitted information and underwriting review. This page is informational and does not guarantee approval, funding amounts, or outcomes.

title: Ecommerce Business Funding

h1: Ecommerce Business Funding for Inventory and Growth

heroImageAlt: Ecommerce operator analyzing sales and inventory reports

Shipping, return activity, and channel fees can reduce available working capital even when top-line sales are healthy. Funding can help operators execute growth plans while maintaining day-to-day operational stability.
financingNeedsIntro: Common ecommerce funding uses include: - Inventory purchases ahead of promotions`n- Digital advertising and acquisition spend`n- Fulfillment and logistics costs`n- Working capital for operating continuity
qualificationIntro: Applicants should provide recent business bank statements, revenue records, and current expense context. A clear use-of-funds plan tied to inventory, marketing, and fulfillment priorities improves review readiness.
processOverview: Typical review flow: 1. Complete an initial application.`n2. Submit requested documents.`n3. Evaluate available terms if qualified.`n4. Accept terms and complete final funding steps. Turnaround depends on document quality and underwriting requirements.

title: Restaurant Funding

Vendor timing, seasonality, and equipment events can create short-term cash pressure even for established operators. Funding can help maintain staffing and service continuity during uneven operating cycles.

qualificationIntro: Applicants should prepare recent business bank statements, performance history, and recurring expense detail. Documentation around seasonality and planned use of funds helps improve review accuracy.

title: Trucking Business Funding

heroSubhead: Manage fuel, payroll, maintenance, and receivables timing with financing tailored to trucking operations.
heroImageAlt: Trucking business owner reviewing fleet expenses
industryContext: Trucking operators often pay fuel, payroll, maintenance, and insurance costs before receivables are collected. Longer payment cycles and rate variability can strain short-term working capital. Funding can help support consistent dispatch operations and fleet readiness through uneven cash timing.
financingNeedsIntro: Common trucking funding uses include: - Fuel and route operating costs`n- Driver payroll and contractor payouts`n- Maintenance and repair events`n- Working capital for receivables timing gaps
qualificationIntro: Applicants should prepare recent business bank statements, revenue documentation, and operating expense visibility. Adding context on fleet obligations and receivable timing helps support underwriting review.
processOverview: Typical review flow: 1. Submit an application with business details.`n2. Provide requested records for underwriting review.`n3. Review available terms if approved.`n4. Finalize acceptance and funding steps. Timing depends on documentation readiness and underwriting needs.

title: Contractor Business Funding

heroSubhead: Support labor, materials, and project timing requirements with financing aligned to contractor workflows.
